name: Hunyuan-T1
archDisplay: Hybrid Transformer-Mamba MoE deep-reasoning model built on TurboS base (params undisclosed)
note: Mamba-powered deep-reasoning flagship built on TurboS, API-only via Tencent Cloud/Yuanbao; scores 87.2 MMLU-Pro and 96.2 MATH-500, close to DeepSeek R1 and trailing only o1.

results.notes: AIME 2024. Tencent's own official comparison table (per the source article, this and the following rows are explicitly Tencent's official published figures, not the outlet's own eval) is rendered as an image on the primary tencent.github.io page and could not be OCR'd directly, but this figure is independently corroborated verbatim by a second outlet, SCMP: https://www.scmp.com/tech/big-tech/article/3303456/tencents-hunyuan-t1-ai-reasoning-model-rivals-deepseek-performance-and-price
Independently corroborated verbatim by SCMP's coverage of the same official table.
DROP F1; article notes T1 achieves the highest score on this metric among the models compared.

verificationNotes:
Confirmed real release: Tencent officially launched Hunyuan-T1 on 2025-03-21 (WinBuzzer, MarketScreener, AnalyticsVidhya all corroborate). Fetched the official Tencent GitHub model card directly and confirmed all five benchmark numbers (MMLU-Pro 87.2, GPQA-Diamond 69.3, MATH-500 96.2, LiveCodeBench 64.9, Arena-Hard 91.9) literally appear there. Confirmed Hybrid-Transformer-Mamba MoE architecture built on TurboS base, API-only/proprietary availability, and 256K context length via independent search (apxml.com, analyticsvidhya). Params remain undisclosed as stated.

2026-07-16 core-benchmark gap check (mmlu, hle, humaneval, swe-bench, mmmu): checked all three source tiers -- (1) official Tencent launch materials, fetching both the rendered tencent.github.io/llm.hunyuan.T1 page and the raw GitHub README_EN.md directly; (2) independent coverage (WinBuzzer's detailed transcription of Tencent's own comparison table, which lists 13 benchmarks -- MMLU-Pro, GPQA-Diamond, C-SimpleQA, DROP, Zebra-Logic, MATH-500, AIME2024, LiveCodeBench, C-Eval, CMMLU, ArenaHard, CFBench, CELLO, T-Eval -- plus SCMP and MarkTechPost's separate write-ups of the same table); (3) sibling Hunyuan releases (Hunyuan-A13B, Hunyuan-TurboS, Hy3) and Epoch AI / Vals.ai / SuperCLUE search coverage. None of plain MMLU, HLE, HumanEval, or SWE-bench appear in any of these for Hunyuan-T1 specifically -- T1 (released 2025-03-21, before HLE was in common use and before Tencent began running SWE-bench on its models, first seen for Tencent only with Hy3 in mid-2026) never had a dedicated technical report (unlike TurboS's arxiv 2505.15431) and its only public benchmark disclosure is the one comparison table already fully captured in this corpus entry. Treating all four as genuine non-disclosures, not gaps in our search. MMMU is architecturally inapplicable: Hunyuan-T1 is confirmed text-only (modalities: ["text"], capabilities.vision: false) -- Tencent's multimodal/MMMU-eligible evaluations belong to the separate "Hunyuan-T1-Vision" model, not this one.

2026-07-18 round-22 re-chase of hle/swe-bench/mmmu (per updated brief framing these as "current, actively-reported" benchmarks worth searching harder for): re-ran targeted searches across 5 of the 6 prescribed methods and found no new evidence, confirming the 2026-07-16 conclusion still holds.

(1) Official materials: re-fetched tokenmix.ai's 2026 retrospective review of Hunyuan-T1 (which explicitly aggregates "all benchmark results provided for Hunyuan-T1") -- confirms only MMLU-Pro/MATH-500/LiveCodeBench/GPQA-Diamond/AIME are ever cited for this model; no HLE, SWE-bench, or MMMU figure exists in any Tencent-authored source.
(2) Independent trackers: searched Epoch AI benchmarking hub, llm-stats.com, and Vals.ai directly for "Hunyuan-T1" -- none returned a dedicated leaderboard row or score for this model on HLE, SWE-bench Verified, or MMMU (Vals.ai in particular only runs domain-specific legal/tax/finance benchmarks and has never covered this model at all).
(3) Academic sources: arXiv search for "Hunyuan-T1" baseline tables turned up only the Hunyuan-TurboS (2505.15431), Hunyuan-MT (2509.05209), and HY-MT1.5 (2512.24092) technical reports -- TurboS is T1's base model and MT/MT1.5 are unrelated translation-specialist siblings; none use Hunyuan-T1 itself as a cited baseline on HLE/SWE-bench/MMMU.
(4) Sibling-model comparison: checked Tencent's newest flagship launch, Hunyuan 3.0 / Hy3 (July 2026) -- its own launch materials report Hy3's SWE-bench Verified (78.0) and HLE-with-tools (53.2) scores compared against DeepSeek-V4-Pro and GLM-5.2, but never cite Hunyuan-T1's score on either benchmark as a comparison point, confirming T1 was never run through these evals even retrospectively by its own successor's launch team.
(5) Confirmed via corpus file itself that Hunyuan-T1 modalities=["text"] and capabilities.vision=false, reaffirming MMMU is architecturally inapplicable (text-only), not merely unpublished.

No git history shows T1 receiving new benchmark entries in round-21 (92a96d8) either -- consistent with these being genuine non-disclosures rather than an under-searched gap. Conclusion unchanged: hle and swe-bench are real non-disclosures for this specific model (T1 predates HLE's common adoption and Tencent's SWE-bench reporting era, and never received its own technical report where such evals would appear); mmmu remains architecturally inapplicable. All three left as omitted_no_evidence.
